scenes.text: In this scene, we introduce the basic structure of a JavaScript function. A JavaScript function is a given set of statements that performs a task or calculates a value. Functions in JavaScript are a fundamental building block. This scene covers function declaration, where the keyword `function` is followed by the name of the function and a set of parentheses. This will help learners understand how JavaScript functions are structured and the role they play in programming.

This scene delves into the workings of a JavaScript function when invoked. It describes how a function returns a value when called in the code, highlighting the mechanics of passing arguments and retrieving a result. It helps learners see the connection between a function's implementation and its execution in a program, emphasizing practical use cases.
